payload.summary: Adversarial review request for all 33 published blog posts at blog.unratified.org. Two-part scope: (1) factual/rhetorical review using existing AR rubric + defensibility methodology, (2) ethical marketing rubric construction + application.

context.review_pipeline: Three-stage pipeline: unreviewed -> ai-reviewed -> reviewed (human). All 33 posts currently 'unreviewed'. No agent reviews its own work. Each review populates structured audit trail in frontmatter (reviewer, date, status, findings, findingsUrl). Agents index page at /agent/ shows review counts.

related_sessions.persuasion-audit: Turn 1 sent (6 questions Q-A through Q-F, still awaiting response). Q-B (PSQ-Lite full site scan) and Q-F (motivated reasoning mitigation) overlap. Feel free to consolidate.
related_sessions.site-defensibility-review: Turn 3 complete. 12 findings, 4 immediate fixes applied. Your defensibility methodology is the model for this review.

part_1.label: Factual accuracy + source quality + adversarial register

E_psychology_authored.note: Self-review limitation applies. Still check factual accuracy + sources, but AR scoring of your own rhetorical choices has a validity ceiling.

part_2.label: Ethical marketing and advertising rubric — construct then apply
part_2.request: Build a scoring rubric for ethical marketing/advertising compliance (same methodology as AR rubric: literature-grounded dimensions, weighted scoring, worked examples, validation criteria). Then apply to all 33 posts.
suggested_grounding: FTC Act Section 5 (deception/unfairness, substantiation doctrine, net impression test)
ICC Advertising and Marketing Communications Code
AI-generated content disclosure (FTC guidance, emerging state laws)
constraints: Site is advocacy, not commercial — but advocacy can violate ethical marketing standards
Rubric should be reusable for AI-generated advocacy content generally
Distinguish violations (must fix) from concerns (worth discussing)
Include worked examples from our corpus

payload.cadence: Batch A first. Remaining at your pace. Quality over speed.

epistemic_flags: Suggested grounding sources for Part 2 are our best guess — you may identify better frameworks
Self-review limitation on psychology-authored posts acknowledged but not fully resolved
